name: Cable Front Raise
resistance_profile: A low pulley keeps near-constant tension on the front delt through the whole range, unlike dumbbells that unload at the bottom.
cues: Keep the cable running just past the body for constant tension
Raise to shoulder height, no higher
Control the lowering phase against the cable
execution_steps: Set the pulley to the lowest position and attach a straight bar or rope.
Stand facing away from the pulley so the cable runs up from behind, and hold the handle in front of the thighs.
Brace, keep a slight elbow bend, and raise the handle forward to shoulder height.
Pause at the top, keeping tension on the delt.
Lower under control, resisting the cable back to the start.
good_when: You want the constant tension dumbbells cannot give
Adding controlled isolation volume for the front delt
You want a smooth resistance curve that is easy to take to failure
bad_when: Your front delts are already fatigued from pressing
You specifically want to train the deep-stretch position
common_errors: Leaning back to heave the weight up
Letting the stack pull the arm down fast
Using the traps to shrug the weight up
progressions: Switch to single-arm for a longer, even-tension range
regressions: Reduce the load on the stack
Use a light dumbbell front raise
